summary: Audit Log — archival to S3 Glacier after 90 days
description_md:
As a compliance-officer, I want the system to automatically archive audit logs to S3 Glacier after 90 days of retention, so that I can maintain cost-effective long-term storage while ensuring regulatory compliance for all historical data.

**Background:**
The Compliance Vault module captures detailed audit logs for all PII access and modification events across connected data sources. To optimize storage costs while maintaining compliance, these logs must be transitioned to cold storage (S3 Glacier) after the standard 90-day retention period.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
1. Audit logs older than 90 days are automatically identified and moved to S3 Glacier storage with appropriate lifecycle policies configured.
2. Archived audit logs remain queryable via the Audit Log Export feature with a maximum retrieval latency of 4 hours.
3. The workspace-admin can configure custom retention periods per tenant, with a minimum of 30 days and maximum of 365 days.
4. A retention summary report is generated daily showing the count of logs archived, storage savings, and any failed archival operations.

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Testing complete — verified archival lifecycle policy triggers correctly at 90-day threshold. S3 Glacier Deep Archive storage class configured for cost optimization.
